mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures aggregate consumer demand strength; higher demand directly translates into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of RSAFS momentum remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top quintile of RSAFS growth fails to outperform the bottom quintile in subsequent 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5 percent signals restrictive monetary conditions that raise discount rates and debt-service costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5 percent, or the triggered signal Sharpe ratio is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate accelerating labor market deterioration; the joint signal compresses corporate earnings expectations and triggers risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of UNRATE and PAYEMS produces a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals recovering manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side efficiency gains; in early-cycle expansions this translates into operating leverage and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months classified as early-cycle expansion, INDPRO momentum fails to generate positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5, or the strategy hit rate falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) proxies aggregate domestic demand and income generation; positive surprises in this series lead to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of GDPC1 growth remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP surprises do not correspond to statistically significant positive 20-day excess returns.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals a tight labor market that increases wage bargaining power and input-cost pressures; this margin compression narrative dominates earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 5.5 percent, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a stagflationary monetary bind where policy remains restrictive despite rising prices; the joint effect raises real borrowing costs and compresses valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term achieves a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint condition fails to predict negative returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal durable demand strength in the residential construction sector with positive spillovers to employment and credit growth; this demand impulse drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of HOUST changes remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive HOUST surprises do not coincide with positive 5-day returns in more than 52 percent of instances.
